reasoning: Query asks specifically about GI-protected awamori and its production areas. The GI record returned is for Ryukyu Moromi-su (a vinegar byproduct), not awamori itself. A scraped Naha city page provides rich cultural context about awamori (15th-century origins, Okinawa distilleries, two styles) but it surfaces buried amid nav-chrome and unrelated content about tuna and herbs. The core GI ask—awamori itself is GI #44? No, moromi-su is—goes unanswered since awamori the spirit is not listed as its own GI item here. Item 3 (Urasoe Castle map) is noise.
missing_data: GI designation entry for awamori-the-spirit itself (if one exists); production area breakdown by island (Miyako, Yaeyama, Okinawa main island); named distilleries per region beyond the 7 Naha breweries mentioned.
improvement_hint: Add keyword-to-GI cross-reference so a search for '泡盛' surfaces any GI tied to the spirit directly. Filter out off-topic scraped items (castle maps) before returning; de-duplicate body_paragraphs which repeat the same paragraph verbatim multiple times.

reasoning: Query is about Kyoto tofu and shojin-ryori vegetarian experiences. The keyword '豆腐' returned only 2 items: (1) a guided bus-tour article about Ohara/Hiei that mentions yudofu and shojin-ryori in passing deep inside a very long body, and (2) the response is truncated before item 2 is shown. Neither item directly addresses the user's intent. The top result is a sightseeing bus tour article, not a food resource. Core data—dedicated tofu restaurants, Kyoto-style yudofu establishments, shojin-ryori temples—is absent.

improvement_hint: Index body_paragraphs for food-keyword relevance so that food-focused queries surface articles whose primary topic is the food, not incidental mentions inside tourism round-up pages. Also consider scraping dedicated food/restaurant portal pages.

reasoning: Query asks about Toyama Bay shiro-ebi (white shrimp) and Toyama Black ramen. The response has 135 items. The top MAFF GI items are Nyuzen jumbo watermelon, Toyama dried persimmon, and Himi Inazumi plum—none of these are the requested items. The scraped Toyama City Tourism site mentions masuzushi and 'bounties of Toyama Bay' but not shiro-ebi specifically. Toyama Black ramen and shiro-ebi are not in the visible results, suggesting either they lack GI status (reasonable) or the retrieval order buries relevant items. Missing the primary focus items.
missing_data: Shiro-ebi entry or note that it lacks GI status; Toyama Black ramen context; hotaru-ika (firefly squid) which is another iconic Toyama Bay product; any scraped pages specifically about these dishes.
improvement_hint: When a query contains specific named foods (白エビ、ブラックラーメン), boost items that textually match those terms in name or description fields before returning GI items that only match the prefecture. Add a note when queried items are absent from GI registry.

reasoning: Query in Arabic asks about Nara's officially designated agricultural products. The response returns only 1 GI item: Miwa Somen (correctly identified, GI #12), with a high-quality Arabic translation. The see_also Wikidata venues are museums, irrelevant to a food query. Nara has other GI products (Yoshino cedar, Yamato tea) that may not appear here due to category filter. Coverage is thin for a prefecture-level food overview.
missing_data: Yamato cha (tea) GI if it exists; Yoshino cedar-related products; traditional Nara confectionery like 奈良漬 (narazuke pickles); any scraped municipal food pages for Nara.
improvement_hint: For get_local_specialty with category=food and no keyword, ensure all GI items for the prefecture are returned regardless of registration number. The see_also Wikidata venues should be filtered to food-relevant kinds (market, brewery, etc.) when the query is food-focused.

reasoning: Query asks about Miyazaki chicken (jidori) and Miyazaki beef GI protection. The response returns 3 GI items: Miyazaki Beef (GI #55) with detailed award history, Yamatai Kansho sweet potato (GI #64), and Mera Itomaki Daikon (GI #173). Miyazaki Beef is the primary ask and is well-covered with quality descriptors and competition history. The jidori (chicken) is not GI-listed here, which may be accurate. The see_also Wikidata venues are off-topic museums but do not detract significantly.
missing_data: Miyazaki Jidori chicken—note if not GI-listed and why; Hyuga-natsu citrus which is another Miyazaki specialty; context on why jidori differs from regular chicken.
improvement_hint: When a named product (地鶏) is queried but not found in GI registry, add a structured note explaining its absence. Filter see_also venues to food-adjacent kinds when query is clearly food-focused.

reasoning: Query asks about natto and fermented foods from Ibaraki with official designation. The keyword '発酵' (fermentation) returned 2 items: both are scraped text about a local tourism blog (Ryugasaki area walks) with only an incidental mention of a fermentation festival in a neighboring town (Kozaki in Chiba). Neither item covers Ibaraki natto (e.g., Mito natto) or formally designated fermented products. Core data is completely missing. The response wastes all capacity on irrelevant blog content.
missing_data: Mito natto or any Ibaraki natto GI entry; Ibaraki miso or soy sauce products; official food designation for fermented goods; even a keyword match on 納豆 would be more relevant.
improvement_hint: The keyword '発酵' should also trigger a fallback search on related terms (納豆、味噌、醤油) within the same prefecture when primary results are poor. Consider adding a relevance threshold so blog-style scraped pages with only incidental keyword mentions rank below dedicated food product pages.

reasoning: Query asks about Aichi hatcho miso and miso-based dishes (misokatsu, miso nikomi udon). The response returns 39 items. Top item is GI #49 for Hatcho Miso with excellent cultural context. The second item is a detailed article from Okazaki city tourism covering the full history, production method, two rival breweries (Maruya and Kakukyu) with visit hours and addresses, Tokugawa family connection, and modern applications. This is exactly what the query needs. Highly specific, grounded, and practically useful.

improvement_hint: Minor: The scraped article body is heavily duplicated across multiple paragraphs (same text repeated). De-duplication of body_paragraphs before return would reduce noise and improve expression quality.

reasoning: Query in Indonesian asks about officially protected traditional Japanese confectionery (wagashi). The response returns 0 items. The see_also Wikidata venues (Tomioka Silk Mill, Tomonoura, Itsukushima, Siebold Museum, Shuri Castle) are entirely unrelated to wagashi—they appear to be generic popular-spots fallback with no food relevance. The corpus likely contains GI items for wagashi-related products (chinsuko, Yamae kuri chestnuts) but the Japanese keyword '和菓子' failed to match them because those items describe ingredients/products, not wagashi by name.

reasoning: Query asks about Hokkaido jingisukan culture, lamb origins, and eating styles. The response has 44 items. The top item is a detailed history page from Takikawa city covering jingisukan origins (Takikawa breeding farm, WWI wool shortage), the two eating styles (Sapporo-style tare-after vs Takikawa-style pre-marinated), Matsuo Jingisukan history, and April 29 'Lamb Day' designation. The second item covers Goryokaku cherry-blossom jingisukan tradition. Third item provides Sapporo overview mentioning jingisukan alongside ramen and soup curry. Very good coverage of the cultural and culinary aspects of the query.

improvement_hint: Body paragraph de-duplication would help (the Goryokaku item has extensive off-topic content about the tower and surrounding restaurants). Consider extracting food-specific paragraphs when the scraped page covers multiple topics.

reasoning: Query in Portuguese asks about Tottori food specialties, mentioning Matsuba crab. Response has 658 total items (500 shown). First three are MAFF GI items: Tottori Dune Rakkyo (GI #11), Daisen Broccoli (GI #70), Koge Hanagosho Kaki (GI #72), and Daiei Watermelon (GI #80)—all with good Portuguese translations. Matsuba crab (松葉ガニ) is not present in the visible portion, likely missing GI designation or not surfaced in first items. The Portuguese translations are accurate and useful. Overall coverage of Tottori GI products is good even if the specific queried item needs scrolling to find.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Kagoshima kurobuta (black pig), its GI characteristics and branding history. The response returns 0 items—the '黒豚' keyword search returned nothing from the GI or specialty corpus for Kagoshima. The see_also venues are museums (Reimeikan, Chiran Samurai Residences, Kagoshima City Museum) with no food relevance. Kurobuta/Berkshire pig is a famous Kagoshima specialty; its absence suggests either it lacks national GI status or the search failed. Practically useless for the query.

reasoning: Query asks about sake brewing in Fushimi, Kyoto, and designated sake varieties. The keyword '日本酒' returned 6 items. reasoning: Query asks about Yamanashi hoto (flat noodle hot pot) and its official recognition. The response has 64 items. The top item is an excellent Yamanashi tourism portal article with detailed descriptions of hoto restaurants across multiple regions (Kofu, Katsunuma, Fuji Goko, Fujikawa), specific restaurant names and access info, historical context (Takeda Shingen's field ration), variations (Ozara, Mimi), and unique preparations. A second item appears to duplicate this. This is an excellent response to the query—comprehensive, grounded, specific, practically usable. The question of formal official recognition (e.g. GI) is not explicitly answered but implied as cultural designation.

reasoning: Query in Russian asks about official traditional dishes of Osaka. Response has 460 items. Top MAFF GI items are: Tondabayashi Ebi-imo taro (GI #137) and Senshu Mizunasu eggplant (GI #161), both with accurate Russian translations. The third item is an Osaka tourism spot listing page with empty body_paragraphs—not useful. Coverage is good but the user-facing items that directly answer 'what are Osaka dishes' are buried after GI items for vegetables. Takoyaki/okonomiyaki lack GI designations so they won't appear in formal records.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Echizen Crab's GI designation. Response returns exactly 1 GI item: Echizen Gani (GI #69) with full official description covering freshness, cold-chain management, quality characteristics, imperial tribute history (90+ years), national reputation, and premium pricing. This is a near-perfect response to the query—directly answers the GI question with authoritative MAFF data. The see_also Wikidata venues are museums and preservation districts, irrelevant to the query, but they do not pollute the primary result.

improvement_hint: For food-specific GI queries with a clear single result, suppress the see_also Wikidata venues or filter to food/market kinds only. Add fishing season metadata to seafood GI entries.

reasoning: Query asks about officially designated wagashi (Japanese confectionery) across Japan. Response returns 3 GI items: Yamae Kuri chestnuts (Kumamoto, GI #151), Chinsuko (Okinawa, GI #155), and Okinawa Kokuto black sugar (Okinawa, GI #172). All have high-quality English translations and clear descriptions. The results are accurate and grounded. However, only 3 items returned for a national query on 菓子—coverage feels thin. Kagawa wasanbon, Kyoto nama-gashi, and similar products may not have national GI status, which is the honest answer, but context would help.
missing_data: Note that most traditional wagashi lack national GI protection despite cultural prominence; Nagasaki Castella if GI-designated; any regional confectionery GI items that might be missed by '菓子' keyword.
improvement_hint: For national-scope confectionery queries, also search '和菓子', '焼菓子', '銘菓', '饅頭' to broaden coverage. A note clarifying that GI registry covers mainly ingredients/raw products rather than finished confectionery would set appropriate expectations.

reasoning: Query asks for Hirosaki Park cherry blossom timing and 'hana-ikada' (flower raft on moat) viewing season. The response correctly returns 弘前公園 (Wikidata, sakura_meisho_100 certified, tier:must_see) with phone and website. However, the response contains no timing information—no mention of peak bloom dates, hana-ikada timing (late bloom after petals fall, typically late April), or when to visit. Three municipal scrape items are present but without descriptions that contain timing data. The core temporal question is unanswered.
missing_data: Peak bloom dates (typically late April); hana-ikada viewing window (after peak, when petals fall onto moat water); Hirosaki Park apple tree pruning technique that creates longer bloom period; evening illumination information.
improvement_hint: For seasonal queries (桜、紅葉、雪), enrich spot records with seasonal metadata (peak_season, bloom_window) sourced from DMO or municipal pages. The get_spots tool should surface description content containing timing keywords when q='桜'.

reasoning: Query asks for Tokyo cherry blossom spots beyond Ueno Park. Top result is a Tokyo tourism portal spring overview (mentions Chidorigafuchi, Yoyogi, Inokashira), which is relevant. But results 2-4 are Tochigi and western Tokyo suburban locations (Musashimurayama), not central Tokyo spots. The query asked 'beyond Ueno' implying alternatives within accessible Tokyo, but Chidorigafuchi (one of the best alternatives) only appears as a brief mention within a long description rather than as a named spot with its own record. Shinjuku Gyoen, Meguro River, and Koganei Park are absent.
missing_data: Named wikidata or scrape records for Chidorigafuchi, Shinjuku Gyoen, Meguro River, Koganei Park, Sumida Park as specific cherry blossom spots; dates and sakura variety information.
improvement_hint: For prefecture-specific seasonal queries like Tokyo sakura, restrict hybrid search to prefecture='東京' to avoid surfacing Tochigi results. Named spot records for major sakura-meisho destinations should be separately indexed.

reasoning: Query asks about Kyoto autumn foliage hidden gems avoiding Arashiyama and Kiyomizudera. The top result is a cherry blossom article (spring, not autumn) about Kyoto hidden spots—wrong season. The DMO calendar item mentions specific autumn foliage spots (Mukoujinja, Enmachi). Item 3 (Enri-an, 厭離庵, Wikidata) is actually a genuine hidden koyo spot—a small temple in Sagano famous for autumn leaves and limited access. Item 4 (Momijidani) is relevant. But the seasonal confusion (top result is about spring sakura) is a reasoning failure—the system matched on '穴場' keyword without season disambiguation.
missing_data: Jakkoin, Rurikoin, Komyoji, Bishamon-do—all off-the-beaten-path koyo spots; explicit autumn timing (mid-Nov to early Dec for Kyoto); Enri-an opening details (limited season).
improvement_hint: Implement season disambiguation in the hybrid search: when query contains '紅葉' (koyo), penalize results primarily about cherry blossom. A seasonal tag on scraped content would enable this filter.

reasoning: Query in Korean asks about Hokkaido winter snow festivals beyond Sapporo Snow Festival. Response returns 0 items from the festivals corpus. The routing hint correctly identifies 'hanabi' (fireworks) concept match but this is a misfire—the query is about 雪 (snow) festivals, not fireworks. The query_intent matched 'kaido' which is also wrong. The 0-item response with routing to get_events for hanabi is a complete retrieval and reasoning failure. Snow festivals like Asahikawa Winter Festival, Sounkyo Ice Festival, and even the Sapporo Snow Festival itself are well-known and should be findable.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival (さっぽろ雪まつり) record; Asahikawa Winter Festival; Sounkyo Ice Festival; any Schema.org Event objects for Hokkaido winter snow events.
wrong_data: The query_intent detected 'kaido' (ancient highway) and 'hanabi' (fireworks) for a snow festival query—both are incorrect concept matches.
improvement_hint: Add '雪まつり' as a recognized festival concept that routes to appropriate data sources. Fix the intent classifier to recognize Korean text asking about winter snow events rather than misidentifying 'kaido' or 'hanabi'. Scrape Hokkaido DMO winter festival pages.

reasoning: Query asks for best autumn foliage timing and dramatic spots in Nikko. Response correctly returns Nikko-area spots via Tochigi/Nikko filter. Top spots include Tamozawa Imperial Villa (park, rekishi_park_100) with opening hours and fee info—relevant to koyo. Tobu World Square and Nikko Edomura are theme parks, less relevant to koyo. Italian Embassy Villa at Lake Chuzenji is a good koyo spot. However, the core koyo destinations—Nikko Toshogu, Ryuzu Falls, Kegon Falls, Irohazaka road—are absent or not in the truncated response. No timing data (peak usually mid-October at altitude, late October at lower elevation) is provided.
missing_data: Toshogu Shrine koyo records; Kegon Falls; Ryuzu Falls; Irohazaka road koyo; timing windows for different altitude zones (5th station peak vs Nikko town peak).
improvement_hint: Enrich spot records for known koyo destinations with seasonal timing metadata. For koyo queries, prioritize spots with 'park', 'waterfall', 'valley', 'temple', 'shrine' kinds over theme parks in ranking.

reasoning: Query asks about enjoying Nagatoro cherry blossoms and river rafting in the same season. Response correctly surfaces 長瀞渓谷 (Nagatoro Valley, sakura_meisho_100, heritage designated, tier:must_see) as the top wikidata result. Other results are mixed: a 'residents' handbook', a Saitama gift shop guide, and a castle are less relevant. 宝登山 (Mt. Hodo) and 宝登山神社 (Hodosan Shrine) are relevant secondary spots. But the key seasonal information—cherry blossoms peak late March to mid-April, river rafting (荒川ライン下り) operates spring through autumn—is absent. The combination question is not addressed.
missing_data: Arakawa river rafting (荒川ライン下り) spot record or mention; spring bloom dates for Nagatoro area; combination itinerary hint; what specific cherry tree species line the valley.
improvement_hint: For spot queries with compound experience questions (sakura + activity), surface activity-related municipal scrape items that co-mention both. Add seasonal metadata to sakura_meisho_100 spots. The 'residents' handbook' and gift-shop results should be deprioritized for tourism queries.

reasoning: Query in Chinese (Traditional) asks about best season for Mt. Fuji snow scenery and best Fuji Five Lakes photography angle. Top result is a Yamagata tourism page (snow monsters, not Fuji)—completely off-target. Second result is a Yamanashi tourism portal with Fuji Five Lakes mention including a relevant passage about snow-capped Fuji with cherry blossoms. Fourth result (富士山絶景ポイント from Yaizu) describes Fuji viewpoints from the ocean/coast, which is useful. The Chinese Traditional text is handled. However, Fuji Five Lakes specific photography angles (Kawaguchiko, Yamanakako, Motosuko) are not specifically addressed and the top result is wrong.
missing_data: Kawaguchiko vs Yamanakako vs Motosuko photography angle comparison; Chureito Pagoda as iconic snow Fuji viewpoint; best months for snow cap (Oct-June, with Dec-Feb fullest); Fuji Five Lakes named individually with coordinates.
improvement_hint: For Mt. Fuji queries, prefecture-filter to Yamanashi/Shizuoka to avoid matching Yamagata snow content. Add Fuji Five Lakes named entity records with photography-season metadata. The hybrid search should down-rank results from geographically distant prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks about Tohoku summer fireworks festivals and whether Akita Kanto Matsuri and fireworks can be enjoyed together. Response returns 0 festival items. The routing_hint correctly identifies '花火' concept and suggests using get_events instead—this is proper behavior. However, the response provides no data at all about Akita festivals; even a note about Kanto Matsuri (UNESCO-listed, August) being a different type from fireworks would be helpful. The hanabi routing hint with the arg_template is useful meta-information for the agent, but the user gets nothing substantive.
missing_data: Akita Kanto Matsuri data (even a note about its August timing, UNESCO status); Omagari Fireworks (大曲の花火) record which is one of Japan's largest fireworks competitions; any Schema.org Event objects for Akita summer festivals.
improvement_hint: When get_festivals returns 0 items but routing_hint exists, also attempt get_events automatically and include preliminary results. Scrape Akita's major summer festival pages (Omagari fireworks, Kanto Matsuri, Noshiro Neburi Nagashi). Omagari fireworks is a critical gap.

reasoning: Query in French asks about Tohoku cherry blossom viewing, best places. Tool searched Iwate only (one of 6 Tohoku prefectures). Top results are: '桜情報2023ー県南エリア' (2023 cherry info for southern Iwate) duplicated across 3 different municipalities with identical spot_id and description—a deduplication bug. The underlying article from iwatetabi.jp is relevant but stale (2023 data). Other results are generic spot/activity listings. No named cherry blossom spots are returned. The query asked about Tohoku broadly, but only Iwate was searched. Kitakami Tenshochi, Kakunodate (Akita), Hirosaki (Aomori) are all absent.
missing_data: Named cherry blossom spots in Iwate (Kitakami Tenshochi, Morioka Shidare-zakura); cross-prefecture Tohoku coverage (Aomori Hirosaki, Akita Kakunodate, Miyagi Shiroishi); peak bloom timing for Tohoku (late April to early May).
improvement_hint: Deduplicate records with same spot_id before returning results. For regional queries (Tohoku) that specify a single prefecture, add cross-prefecture cherry blossom data from neighboring prefectures. Update 2023-dated seasonal articles or annotate with data freshness warning.
